shorts.title: What warming waters mean for NEPA anglers, environment
shorts.title_distilled: What warming waters mean for NEPA anglers, environment.
shorts.description: PARADISE TOWNSHIP, MONROE COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Species like trout need cold water to survive, and across Pennsylvania, that water is getting harder to find. Warming temperatures are stressing fish, shrinking habitats, and threatening an industry that drives tourism across this state. 28/22 News Reporter Heidi Martens took a closer look at what this means for [...]
